semanticCore: Palm of hand, edge, restraint, check, or entirety
primaryMeaning: Palm of hand or to restrain
quranicRelevance: Restraining hands of enemies ('kaffa aydiyahum'), palm/hand (Kaff), and entirety/all together (Kaffah - 'ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo odkhuloo fee as-silmi kaffatan').

keyDerivations.meaning: To restrain, withhold, avert, turn back, or check
Palm of hand, or scale pan
Entirety, all together, universally, or completely

figurativeOrRareUsages: كَافَّةً لِلنَّاسِ (34:28): Universally/completely for all mankind.
بَاسِطُ كَفَّيْهِ إِلَى المَاءِ: Like one who stretches out his palms/hands to water (futile hope).
discardedNoise: The border hem of a dress (كفة).
Obscure details of hand gestures in greetings.
summaryEn: The root represents constraint and completeness, defining the physical palms, the check on enemy aggression, and the call to enter Islam completely (Kaffah).
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر المنع والشمول، واصفاً كف أيدي المعتدين، وكف اليد الممدودة عبثاً، ودعوة الدخول الجماعي في السلم (كافة).
rawText: إِنْكَفَفَ 7 see 1. حَرْفُ مُكَافَأَةٍ A particle denoting compensation, or the complement of a condition; like حَرْفُ جَزَآءٍ. كَافُّوا عَدُوَّهُمْ 3 i. q. حَاجَزُوهُمْ, which see. === مُكَافَّةٌ signifies i. q. مُحَاجَزَةٌ, because it is a preventing, or an abstaining, from fighting. (Mgh.) كَفَافٌ The like of a thing. (S, K.) === Food, or sustenance, that renders one independent of others: (S, K:) or sufficient for one's want, not exceeding nor falling short. (Msb.) كَفٌّ [generally The hand: sometimes, app., the palm only:] accord. to Az, the palm with the fingers. (Msb.) === [Hence, A cake of the length and thickness of the hand: thus in the present day. See عُجَّالٌ.] === كَفٌّ A handful; what one takes with the hand, or grasps; syn. قُبْضَةٌ. (S, art. قبض.) === [ضَرَبَهُ كَفًّا He struck him a slap with the hand.] === [كَفٌّ often signifies The paw of a beast.] === الكَفُّ الجَذْمَآءُ The star α of Cetus. === الكَفُّ الخَضِيبُ The β of Cassiopeia. كَفَّ التَّوْبَ 1 He sewed the edge, or border, of the garment, or piece of cloth, the second time, (S, K,) after the [slight] sewing termed المَلُّ, (S,) or الشَّلُّ. (K.) === [He felled (a seam or garment).] === كَفَّ عَنْهُ, aor. ﹹ, He refrained, or forbore, from it, as forbidden; abstained, or desisted, from it; left, relinquished, or forsook it: (Msb:) [as also 🡻انكفّ]. === كَفَّ عَنْهُ He refrained, desisted, forbore, abstained, or held, from it. (K, &c.) === كَفَّهُ عَنْهُ He made him to refrain, forbear, or abstain, from it; averted him, turned him away or back, from it; (K;) prevented, hindered, held, withheld, or restrained, him from it. (Mgh, Msb, TA.) === [كَفَّ بَيْنَهُمَا He interposed as a restrainer between them two: a phrase of frequent occurrence]. === كَفَّ مِنَ الشَّعَرِ (M, K, art. قصر) He shortened the hair. (M, ibid.) كَفَّةٌ : see كِفَّةٌ. كُفَّةٌ The selvage, i. e. border, or side, of a garment or piece of cloth, (S, Msb,) that has no هُدْب [or end of unwoven threads]: (S, voce طُرَّةٌ:) or what surrounds the skirt of a shirt: or whatever is oblong; as the حَاشِيَة of a garment or piece of cloth, and of sand: and the edge of a thing. (K.) كِفَافٌ The circuit, rim, or surrounding edge, of a thing. (S, K.) كِفَافَةٌ [app. a subst., not an inf. n.,] The act, or art, of sewing in the manner termed كَفٌّ; (TA;) contr. of شِلَاشَةٌ. (TA in art. شل.) كِفَّةٌ The bezel, or collet, i. e. the part in which the stone is set, of a signet-ring. (TA in art. ركب.) See كُرْسِىٌّ. === See also طَرْقٌ. === كِفَّةٌ A scale of a balance: (MA:) vulg. 🡻كَفَّةٌ. (K.) نَاقَةٌ كَافَّةٌ An old and weak she-camel. (AO, TA in art. سدم.) === [مَا كَافَّةٌ The restrictive مَا, which is annexed to أَنَّ إِنَّ, &c.: so called because it restrains the particle to which it is adjoined from exercising any government.] === كَافَّةً Wholly. (Bd and Jel in ii. 204.)
